page: Microsoft Azure M32bs_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MBSMediumMemoryv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8473C processor operating at 4.0 GHz. The x86_64 architecture is supported by a 105 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and AVX-512. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance includes no local storage, but it is premium storage capable with block storage performance.

In benchmark evaluations, the M32bs_v3 demonstrates strong memory and processing capabilities. Uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth reached 182.67 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% of tested servers, while cached small-block reads achieved 2764.98 GB/sec. Single-core performance is highly competitive, with Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing scores all ranking in the top 10%. Multi-threaded operations also show high efficiency, as evidenced by a strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 210.74 GB/sec and a static web serving throughput of 53.69 GB/sec.

Despite its strong CPU and memory subsystems, the server exhibits clear performance tradeoffs. Large-scale LLM text generation using a 70B model is weak, yielding only 2.74 tokens/sec, though small 135M model text generation is highly efficient at 343.5 tokens/sec. The lack of local storage requires reliance on external block storage. Given its high memory-to-core ratio and strong single-core and memory bandwidth metrics, the M32bs_v3 is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, text and image processing pipelines, and web serving applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure M32bs_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MBSMediumMemoryv3 family, powered by a dedicated 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8473C processor running at 4.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 256.0 GB of RAM, offering a high resource density of 8.0 GB per core. While it lacks local storage, it is premium storage capable. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing scores in the top 10%. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is strong at 182.67 GB/sec. However, text generation for large 70B LLM models is a notable tradeoff, performing in the bottom 25%. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, text and image processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M32bs_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It delivers top-tier single-core performance but lacks local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M32bs_v3 is a 32-vCPU, 256 GB RAM memory-optimized server with an Intel Xeon 4.0 GHz processor and no local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and dense RAM configurations for demanding database and processing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM powered by a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon 8473C processor.
Top-tier single-core, image processing, and text processing benchmark performance.
Strong uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 182.67 GB/sec.
No local storage included, requiring external premium block storage.
Well-suited for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and data processing.
